seoTitle: How to Add a Logo in Typeform for Branded Forms
metaDescription: Learn how to upload and customize your logo in Typeform to build a consistent, branded look across your surveys and forms.
intro: A form without branding can feel generic, especially when it's representing your business to customers or leads. This guide covers how to add your logo to a Typeform, match it with your form's theme, and preview the branded result before publishing.

steps.heading: Open the Design Settings
steps.body: Go to the design section of your Typeform editor where branding and theme options are managed.

steps.heading: Upload your logo
steps.body: Add your company or personal logo file so it appears on the form's welcome screen and throughout the experience.

steps.heading: Customize branding and theme
steps.body: Adjust colors and layout to complement your logo, creating a consistent visual experience for respondents.

steps.heading: Preview and publish your form
steps.body: Check how the branded form looks before publishing to make sure the logo displays correctly across devices.

faq.question: What image formats work for a Typeform logo?
faq.answer: Typeform generally accepts common image formats for logos, so a standard PNG or JPG file should work when uploading.

faq.question: Does adding a logo affect form completion rates?
faq.answer: Branding can improve trust and a sense of professionalism, which may help completion rates, especially for business-facing forms.

faq.question: Can I use different logos for different forms?
faq.answer: Yes, each form's design settings are managed individually, so you can use different logos across different Typeforms.

faq.question: Is a logo required to publish a Typeform?
faq.answer: No, a logo is optional, but it's a simple way to make surveys, quizzes, and lead forms feel more professional and on-brand.
